summary: Russia intensified strikes on energy infrastructure, hitting Naftogaz facilities in Kharkiv and Sumy regions and launching a large‑scale missile and drone barrage including Iskander‑M rockets. Ukrainian forces reported recent territorial gains near Novoselivka in Dnipropetrovsk and continue to defend against Shahed attacks on rail nodes such as Shostka. Kyiv has offered air‑defence assistance to Romania amid cross‑border drone incidents.
key_points: Russia attacked multiple Naftogaz sites in Kharkiv and Sumy, with repeated strikes on a Sumy facility lasting hours (Source: @UkraineNow/81534).
Shahed drones destroyed the Shostka railway building and damaged the station, no casualties reported (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30547).
A combined strike employed an Iskander‑M ballistic missile, six H‑101 cruise missiles and 290 attack drones, with Ukrainian air defence downing 284 targets (Source: @UkraineNow/81548).
Ukrainian forces liberated territory near Novoselivka in Dnipropetrovsk and cleared enemy positions in several villages (Source: @UkraineNow/81535).
A Russian drone hit a multi‑storey building in Romania during attacks on Odesa (Source: @UkraineNow/81545).
Ukraine pledged to help protect Romanian airspace from Russian drones (Source: @UkraineNow/81540).
Night attacks on Taganrog port ignited a tanker, fuel reservoir and administration building (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a coordinated strike on Ukrainian energy assets, hitting Naftogaz facilities in Kharkiv and Sumy and employing Iskander‑M missiles, cruise missiles and hundreds of drones. Ukrainian forces achieved limited ground gains near Novoselivka in Dnipropetrovsk while defending rail infrastructure from Shahed attacks. Kyiv extended air‑defence support to Romania after a cross‑border drone incident.

points: The Ukrainian defence ministry reported that Naftogaz facilities in Kharkiv and Sumy were attacked repeatedly, with a Sumy site struck twice within hours, indicating sustained Russian pressure on energy infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/81534).
Ukrainian rail operator confirmed that Shahed drones destroyed the Shostka railway building and damaged the station, though no workers or passengers were harmed (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30547).

Ukrainian ground forces announced the liberation of territory near Novoselivka in Dnipropetrovsk and ongoing clearing operations in villages such as Voronny, Sichneve, and others, reporting at least 46 km² reclaimed (Source: @UkraineNow/81535).
Kyiv offered to assist Romania in defending its skies against Russian drones, pledging personnel and technical expertise (Source: @UkraineNow/81540).

Russian forces conducted a large‑scale missile and drone attack comprising an Iskander‑M ballistic missile, six H‑101 cruise missiles and 290 Shahed drones, with Ukrainian air defence claiming 284 interceptions (Source: @UkraineNow/81548).
In Taganrog, Russian‑controlled forces suffered a night drone strike that ignited a tanker, a fuel reservoir and an administrative building at the port (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).
A Russian drone struck a multi‑storey building in Romania during the broader campaign against Odesa, marking a cross‑border escalation (Source: @UkraineNow/81545).
Russian officials claimed 127 enemy drones were shot down during the night, reflecting high aerial activity over occupied territories (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).
Shahed drones targeted the Shostka railway facility in Sumy region, causing structural damage but no casualties (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30547).
Ukrainian forces reported successful clearance of enemy positions near Voronny, Sichneve, and other settlements in the eastern Dnipropetrovsk area (Source: @UkraineNow/81535).
Russian missile and drone barrage included strikes on Kharkiv and Sumy energy sites, indicating pressure on the eastern logistical corridor (Source: @UkraineNow/81534).
Ukrainian intelligence noted increased tactical aviation activity from the south‑east, east, and north‑east, heightening threat to front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/63305).
Multiple UAV sightings were reported in Chernihiv region, including near Slavutych and Snovsk, suggesting ongoing reconnaissance (Source: @eRadarrua/94233).
A reactive UAV was observed heading toward Korosten from the east, indicating potential strike intent in northern Zhytomyr (Source: @kpszsu/63292).
UAV activity near the Belarusian border was noted, with a drone moving toward Rokytne, highlighting cross‑border dynamics (Source: @eRadarrua/94265).
Reports of a UAV near Horodnia and Semenivka in Chernihiv region point to continued aerial pressure in the north (Source: @eRadarrua/94286).
Russian drone attacks hit oil infrastructure in the occupied Crimean city of Feodosia, as well as a fuel depot in Armavir, Krasnodar region (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).
UAV sightings in Kherson and Mykolaiv border areas were reported, indicating potential strike vectors toward the southern axis (Source: @eRadarrua/94265).
A UAV was observed near Malokaterynivka and Kushuhum in Zaporizhzhia, suggesting pressure on southern industrial zones (Source: @eRadarrua/94281).
Russian tactical aviation activity was flagged for the south‑east direction, raising risk for frontline positions (Source: @kpszsu/63305).
Ukrainian air defence reported downing 284 aerial targets during the massive Iskander‑M and drone barrage, including five H‑101 missiles and 279 Shahed drones (Source: @UkraineNow/81548).
Russian claims of 127 enemy drones shot down overnight reflect intense aerial engagements over occupied territories (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).
UAV activity was documented across multiple oblasts, including Kharkiv, Chernihiv, and Zaporizhzhia, indicating widespread aerial threats (Source: @eRadarrua/94233, @eRadarrua/94281).

A reported combined strike plan mentioned potential use of 25 H‑101 cruise missiles from Tu‑95MS and Tu‑160 bombers, plus up to 24 Kalibr missiles from Black Sea launchers (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67298).

key_developments: Russia attacked multiple Naftogaz facilities in Kharkiv and Sumy, with a Sumy site hit twice within hours, indicating sustained pressure on Ukrainian energy infrastructure. (Source: @UkraineNow/81534)

A coordinated Russian strike employed an Iskander‑M ballistic missile, six H‑101 cruise missiles and 290 Shahed drones, with Ukrainian air defence claiming 284 interceptions. (Source: @UkraineNow/81548)
Ukrainian forces liberated territory near Novoselivka in Dnipropetrovsk and cleared enemy positions in several villages, reclaiming at least 46 km². (Source: @UkraineNow/81535)
Shahed drones destroyed the Shostka railway building and damaged the station, though no casualties were reported. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30547)
A Russian drone struck a multi‑storey building in Romania during attacks on Odesa, marking a cross‑border escalation. (Source: @UkraineNow/81545)
Ukraine pledged air‑defence assistance to Romania, offering personnel and expertise to protect Romanian skies from Russian drones. (Source: @UkraineNow/81540)
Night drone attacks on Taganrog port ignited a tanker, fuel reservoir and administrative building, highlighting vulnerability of Russian‑held ports. (Source: @UkraineNow/81546)
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are intensifying combined‑arms attacks on critical Ukrainian infrastructure, employing long‑range missiles, cruise missiles and large drone swarms. The eastern and southern fronts face heightened aerial pressure, while northern UAV activity suggests reconnaissance and potential strike preparation. Ukrainian air defences are absorbing significant loads but continue to intercept the majority of incoming threats, maintaining a contested airspace.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is extending defensive cooperation to neighboring Romania, offering air‑defence support after a cross‑border drone incident. International observers note the escalation of Russian strikes on energy assets, prompting diplomatic calls for restraint. Regional partners are monitoring the increased use of long‑range weapons and the potential spill‑over effects on neighboring states.
sections.intelligence_notes: UAV sightings across multiple oblasts indicate a broad surveillance network supporting Russian strike planning. The reported combined strike capabilities, including Tu‑95MS, Tu‑160 and Kalibr missiles, suggest preparation for further large‑scale operations. Ukrainian territorial gains near Novoselivka may signal a localized counter‑offensive, but sustained Russian missile and drone activity continues to challenge front‑line stability.
